name: Frank Zappa
abstract: Frank Vincent Zappa (December 21, 1940 – December 4, 1993) was an American composer, songwriter, guitarist, conductor, actor, satirist, filmmaker, and activist. In a career spanning more than 30 years, Zappa composed rock, pop, jazz, jazz fusion, orchestral and musique concrète works; he additionally produced nearly all the 60-plus album…

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: Glen Ferris, who is a fantastic trombone player that had played with both Frank Zappa and Harry James
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Humphrey
about_quotes.evidence: joined Frank Zappa’s Mothers of Invention for a couple of years
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Humphrey interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: The music was very difficult. He was like a contemporary classical composer that happened to play rock & roll guitar. ... And it was difficult stuff. It was like playing Stravinsky.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: The Beatles, Frank Zappa, all those things in the 60s, all my friends were into that. And I just loved it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: "Hey how’s the new Frank Zappa album?" You know. ... So he was my inside guy to music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe and Christian Goodwillie, via Zoom, June 30, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: the stuff we played, basically the group was sort of like Frank Zappa was what it was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999
